title: Dual Identifier Endpoints (UUID + Slug)
context: Actions serves three interface surfaces: Web (Angular), CLI, and MCP. Web consumers work naturally with UUIDs. CLI and MCP consumers benefit from human-readable slugs — `workflow-definitions/corrective-action-v2` is more ergonomic than `workflow-definitions/a1b2c3d4-...`.
Requiring a separate resolve-by-slug endpoint adds an extra round-trip for CLI/MCP consumers on every operation.
decision: **All entity API endpoints accept both UUID and slug identifiers.** Format detection disambiguates — UUID format is unambiguous (8-4-4-4-12 hex pattern), so anything that doesn't match UUID is treated as a slug.
- Slugs are unique within entity type and tenant scope, enforced by a composite unique index on `(TenantId, Slug)` per entity table
- Slugs are immutable after creation (enforced by EntityMapper — slug property is set on create, intentionally omitted on update). Changing a slug would break CLI scripts and bookmarks.
- Slugs must not parse as valid UUIDs. Validation rejects slugs matching the UUID format pattern.
- Slug format: lowercase-kebab-case, max 128 characters, `[a-z0-9]([a-z0-9-]*[a-z0-9])?` pattern
- Web UI uses UUIDs; CLI and MCP use slugs
- Lookup endpoints return both UUID and slug in responses
consequences: - Single endpoint serves all consumers — no separate slug-resolution API
- CLI/MCP workflows are human-readable and scriptable
- Zero cost for web consumers (UUIDs work unchanged)
- Every entity endpoint has implicit format-detection logic (cross-cutting concern)
- Slug immutability is a permanent constraint — renaming requires creating a new entity
- Query layer must support lookup by either identifier type
**Trade-off accepted:** CLI and MCP are confirmed interface surfaces. The format-detection cost is paid once in the base query infrastructure and amortised across all features.
origin: Identity convention (ADR-0003), adopted by Actions for confirmed CLI/MCP requirement.
